rulings.Case Title: GILBERT VS THE ALLIANCE (UNDER SEAL)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 9, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT HARMON INDUSTRIES, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as the claim is barred by the statute of limitations, Government Code 12654(a). The allegeations of the First Amended Complaint cannot be ignored and demonstrate that a responsible government official became aware of the false claim in 1980. Under Debro vs. Los Angeles Raiders (2001) 92 Cal App 4th, 940, 953, the responsible government official is not the qui tam plaintiff, rather it is a government official who becomes aware of the claim. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ARRIAGA VS. SUPREME CHICKEN RC, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 9, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT FERNANDO BORJA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is granted under CCp 473.5(a) provided defendant pays plaintiff's reasonable costs and attorneys' fees in obtaining the default. If the parties are unable to agree on the amount, plaintiff may file a motion and the Court will determine the amount. The Court's ruling is based on the liberal policy favoring a decision on the merits. The motion is denied under CCP 473 (b) as untimely. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: VANESSA-RENE MONTANO VS. JAMES READ SKAGGS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And/Or Defualt Judgemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 9, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT JAMES SKAGGS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And/Or Default Judgment. The motion is denied. It is untimely, having been filed 3 days beyond the statutory deadline (CCP 1008 and 1013). Moreover, defendant has not presented new facts or law but relies on "facts more fully explained," an argument that is outdated by CCP 1008. Finally, the "facts more fully explained" do not demonstrate mistake, surprise, excusable neglect or inadvertance, particularly given the prior testimony of defendant and Ms. Skaggs in open Court. =(302/PJM/xAM)

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE EVANS VS. THE UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Lift Discovery Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 9, 2008, line 15. PLAINTIFF JESSIE EVANS' Motion To Lift Discovery Stay. The motion is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate what facts plaintiff expects to discover that are not otherwise within plaintiff's knowledge or a part of the record. The Vartain declarations specifically state what he knew and relied upon. Further, the record reflects that plaintiff consented to Gore-Mann's statement regarding a leave and the allegation in the complaint about prior misconduct is vague. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER ANOLICK et al VS. HELEN SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pl'S Mtn For Order Awarding Attorney'S Fees And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 9, 2008, line 18. PLAINTIFFS ALEXANDER ANOLICK and OLGA KOVSHAR PRIVATE CHARITABLE FOUNDATION'S Motion For Order Awarding Attorney'S Fees And For Sanctions is granted. Plaintiff to receive $1000.00 in attorneys' fees in obtaining the default. In seeking relief from default, the only grounds offered was counsel's unavailability. As a consequence, the Court finds this was a motion under CCP 473(b) and fees are mandatory. =(302/PJM/VC)
